channel.title: Octopoda Blog
channel.description: Product updates, engineering deep dives, and guides for building AI agents with persistent memory.

item.title: The Morality of AI in Warfare: Who Is Deploying It, How, and What It Means for All of Us
item.description: AI is selecting bombing targets, compressing kill chains to minutes, and lowering the political cost of war. Here is exactly who is deploying it and what it means.

item.title: AI Agent Failure Modes: What 1,600 Traces Reveal About Loops, Token Waste, and Detection
item.description: Research shows 41-86% of multi-agent systems fail. We break down the data on loops, token waste, and detection methods that actually work.

item.title: 10 AI Experts Predict What Agents Will Look Like by 2030
item.description: Hinton, Amodei, Suleyman, Bengio, and 6 more AI leaders predict the future of AI agents. 8 of 10 outlooks are bleak. Real quotes, real data.

item.title: What Claude Mythos Means for AI Agents (And What to Build Now)
item.description: Claude Mythos scores 93.9% on SWE-bench and finds zero-days autonomously. We break down what this step change means for AI agent developers and why agent memory matters more than ever.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of AI Agent Loops Nobody Is Talking About
item.description: AI agent loops silently burn tokens and cash. Learn how to detect, measure, and stop agent loops before they drain your budget. Real data and code inside.

item.title: When AI Agents Go Rogue: 7 Real Cases That Should Worry Every Developer
item.description: Real cases of AI agents going wrong — from data exfiltration to $47K cloud bills. Learn what failed technically and how to build agents with proper guardrails.

item.title: The Pentagon Wanted Autonomous Agents With No Restrictions. One Company Said No.
item.description: Anthropic refused to give the military unrestricted AI access. They lost a $200M contract and got blacklisted. OpenAI stepped in within hours.

item.title: How Much Money Are Your AI Agents Wasting Without You Knowing It?
item.description: AI agents waste tokens through loops, redundant calls, and context loss. Learn how to detect hidden token waste and cut your LLM costs with real data.

item.title: I Tracked What Actually Happens Inside AI Agents for 30 Days
item.description: 847 loops caught, 23% wasted runtime, agents contradicting each other 31% of the time. Here's what real production data from 70 developers revealed.

item.title: We Analysed 1 Million AI Agent Operations
item.description: Raw operational data from real agents in production. The amnesia tax, loop economics, multi-agent performance gaps, and the brutal token economy — with real numbers.

item.title: How to Add Persistent Memory to a LangChain Agent
item.description: Add LangChain persistent memory to any agent in three lines of Python. Works locally with SQLite, includes semantic search. Free, open source, MIT licensed.

item.title: 10 Things That Could Go Wrong With AI Agents
item.description: From strategic deception to irreversible actions to the accountability gap — here are 10 things that can and do go wrong with AI agents, backed by real research.

item.title: How to Give Your AI Agent Memory in 5 Minutes (With Code)
item.description: Every AI agent forgets everything when the session ends. Here's how to add persistent memory to any Python agent in about 5 minutes with real, working code.

item.title: What Happens When AI Agents Start Remembering Everything
item.description: When we fix the memory problem, we create AI agents that build a permanent, searchable, growing model of everything they interact with.

item.title: Your AI Agent Has Amnesia and You Probably Haven't Noticed Yet
item.description: Every agent framework gives you the same pitch. Autonomous agents that reason, plan, and execute. And it works. Until the session ends.

item.title: What Are AI Agents and Why Every Developer Is Building Them Right Now
item.description: Something shifted in 2025. Developers stopped building chatbots and started building agents. Here's the practical version of what they are, why they matter, and where they fall apart.
